tldr: Hybrid DINOv2-CNN segmentation pipeline for scientific image forgery detection, using frozen transformer features, gradient-enhanced adaptive thresholding, and TTA to produce RLE-encoded masks.
summary: This notebook implements a hybrid forgery detection pipeline combining a frozen DINOv2 vision transformer encoder with a custom CNN decoder for pixel-wise segmentation. It loads a pre-trained model, extracts 768-channel feature maps, and upsamples them to 518x518 probability maps. The inference pipeline applies test-time augmentation (horizontal and vertical flips), followed by a gradient-enhanced adaptive thresholding step that combines raw probabilities with Sobel gradient magnitudes. A grid search over area and mean probability thresholds is performed on a validation split to optimize F1 score. Finally, masks are post-processed with morphological operations and RLE-encoded for Kaggle submission.
extensive_summary:
## Model Architecture & Setup
The notebook defines a `DinoSegmenter` model that freezes a pre-trained DINOv2 base encoder (`AutoModel.from_pretrained`) and attaches a custom `DinoTinyDecoder`. The decoder takes 768-channel DINOv2 feature maps (reshaped from `[B, N, C]` to `[B, C, s, s]`) and passes them through three sequential convolutional blocks (768→384→192→96 channels) with ReLU activations and Dropout2d(0.1). Each block is followed by bilinear interpolation upsampling (37x37 → 74x74 → 148x148 → 296x296). A final 1x1 convolution reduces channels to 1, followed by another bilinear interpolation to the target size of 518x518. Sigmoid activation produces probability maps.

## Data Loading & Preprocessing
Images are loaded from `train_images/authentic`, `train_images/forged`, and `train_masks`. The dataset class resizes images to 518x518, normalizes pixel values to [0,1], and converts `.npy` masks to binary format. The dataset is split 80/20 into train and validation sets using `train_test_split` with `random_state=42`. DataLoaders use batch size 2 and 2 workers.

## Inference & Post-Processing Pipeline
The core inference function `pipeline_final` applies TTA by averaging predictions from the original image, horizontally flipped input, and vertically flipped input. Raw probability maps are passed to `enhanced_adaptive_mask`, which computes Sobel gradients, normalizes them, and fuses them with the probability map using `alpha_grad=0.45` (`enhanced = 0.55*prob + 0.45*grad_norm`). A Gaussian blur (3x3) is applied, followed by thresholding at `mean + 0.3*std`. Morphological closing (5x5) and opening (3x3) refine the mask. The final binary decision uses two thresholds: `AREA_THR` (minimum mask pixels) and `MEAN_THR` (minimum mean probability inside the mask).

## Threshold Tuning & Submission
A grid search sweeps `MEAN_THR` from 0.20 to 0.291 (step 0.01) against a fixed `AREA_THR` of 200 on the validation set. F1 scores are computed per image (with `zero_division=1` for authentic samples) to find the optimal thresholds. Predicted masks are resized to original image dimensions, RLE-encoded using a custom function, or labeled "authentic" if thresholds aren't met. Results are merged with the sample submission CSV and saved.

## Visualization
The notebook includes cells to visualize original images, ground truth masks, and predicted masks for both forged and authentic validation samples, overlaying predictions with alpha blending and printing area/mean/threshold metrics.
post_processing: Gradient-enhanced adaptive thresholding (Sobel + probability fusion), Gaussian blur (3x3), morphological close (5x5) and open (3x3), area/mean probability filtering, grid search for thresholds, RLE encoding
notable_techniques: Frozen DINOv2 feature extraction for segmentation
Gradient-enhanced adaptive thresholding combining probability maps with Sobel gradients
Area and mean probability filtering for binary classification
Grid search on validation set for threshold optimization
Custom RLE encoder for Kaggle submission
